skillName: Perimeter of an isosceles triangle or an isosceles trapezium
misconceptionSummary: Students treat all three sides of an isosceles triangle as equal, using the equilateral formula.

visuals.ruleText: An isosceles triangle has exactly 2 equal sides (legs) and 1 different side (base)

steps.narration: Isosceles means two equal sides — the legs. The third side, the base, is different. The perimeter is two times the leg length plus the base.

lines.text: Isosceles triangle: legs 8 cm, base 5 cm

steps.narration: Two times eight plus five: sixteen plus five equals twenty-one centimetres.

lines.text: Isosceles trapezium: parallel sides 9 cm and 5 cm, legs 4 cm each

steps.narration: An isosceles trapezium has two parallel sides of different lengths and two equal legs. Add both parallel sides and double the leg length: nine plus five plus eight equals twenty-two centimetres.

visuals.expression: Triangle: 2 × leg + base. Trapezium: a + b + 2 × leg
visuals.label: Isosceles = 2 equal sides — identify which sides are equal first

steps.narration: Identify the equal pair first, then build the perimeter formula. Two equal sides plus the remaining different sides.

misconceptionStrip.text: Common mistake: using 3 × side for an isosceles triangle. Isosceles has 2 equal sides, not 3. Use 2 × leg + base — not the equilateral formula.
misconceptionStrip.audioNarration: Isosceles means two equal sides, not three. The base is a different length. Always use 2 × leg + base for an isosceles triangle's perimeter.
